articles.title: Jefferson Lewis arrested over alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Jefferson Lewis has been the subject of a police manhunt, and is accused of murdering the young girl in Alice Springs.

articles.title: Community in mourning after 5yo girl's body found

children.content: Tributes are flowing in Alice Springs for a five-year-old Aboriginal girl whose body was found earlier today, as the community mourns her death.

image.altText: Flowers on a fence
Police steps back with gun.

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby's alleged killer Jefferson Lewis, 47, has been flown to Darwin after violent clashes outside the Alice Springs hospital he had been in.

items.title: Alleged killer of 5yo girl moved to Darwin after violent clashes outside hospital

updates.title: Charges expected to be laid in the coming days

items.title: Bondi royal commission's restrained report still sends a damning message

children.content: The message beneath the restrained language of the report investigating the Bondi terror attack is that by Hanukkah 2025, Australia had grown dangerously complacent about terrorism – and about the safety of the Jewish community.

image.altText: Police officers at Bondi Beach seven days after terrorist attack

items.title: Melbourne and Sydney record largest house price falls across the country

children.content: Momentum in the housing market has slowed, with every capital city recording a slower pace of growth in April.

image.altText: A new housing estate stretches as far as the eye can see.

items.title: Ex students, parents allege 'cult-like' approach at alternative school

children.content: For 50 years, this alternative school sold parents a vision of progressive education. But some who have passed through its doors say a 'cult-like' culture enabled decades of unchecked physical abuse.

gallery.altText: Faye Berryman and Philip O'Carroll in a sepia toned photo with deidentified children.

items.title: The home battery rebate is changing. Is it still worth it and what's the right size?

image.altText: Rooftops with solar panels and a graphic pof batteries

items.title: Crucial interest rate has broken from the global benchmark, costing us billions

children.content: The 10-Year Treasury bond, which the Commonwealth issues to raise money, has pushed past 5 per cent, raising the cost of paying off debt and suggesting inflation is not under control.

articles.title: Australia in talks over latest US proposal to open Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Australia has begun discussions with the United States about joining the latest US proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration tries to build an international coalition to restore freedom of navigation in the critical waterway.

image.altText: Three ships and a small dinghy on a body of water.

articles.title: 'Planning for a rainy day': How China is dealing with the global oil shock

children.content: China relies heavily on Middle Eastern oil imports, but experts say a double-insurance strategy appears to have helped the country absorb at least the initial shock after disruption to the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: An aerial view shows a crude oil tanker at an oil terminal.

children.content: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei says Tehran will eliminate "the enemies' abuses of the waterway" under new management of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

items.title: 'Too hard to prove': Concerns raised after one year of coercive control laws in Queensland
